expectations.text: Response mentions at least 2 of the 5 hero formulas
expectations.evidence: Names all 5 explicitly with section references: Iris-Reveal (§ C), Topic-Hub/Ken-Burns Static (§ B), Filmstrip Wipe (§ D), Sequence Stage (§ E), Lens Shader (§ A).

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend lens shader hero
expectations.evidence: Explicitly excluded as 'exklusiv zu index.html (§ A)'.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend filmstrip wipe hero
expectations.evidence: Explicitly excluded as 'gehört zu Motorrad (§ D)'.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend replacing with static + plain gradient
expectations.evidence: Option 5 explicitly named and rejected: 'Watert die Portrait-Identität auf Stock-Niveau runter. Genau das Failure-Pattern aus SKILL.md Punkt 9.'
@media (prefers-reduced-motion:reduce) block in CSS with explicit fallback states.
data-theme="dark" and data-header-theme="dark" on the section.
Full HTML + scoped CSS block at end of response, ~50 lines of CSS plus markup.

Three-point justification: (1) iris is the portrait section's signature per § C; (2) actual problem is stack of 6 concepts, not the iris; (3) canonical pattern is one __photo, not slide-stack — current is overgrown.

expectations.text: Code skeleton uses var(--ease), clamp(), dark section markers
expectations.evidence: var(--ease), var(--accent-2), var(--muted), var(--fg), clamp() throughout, data-theme/data-header-theme attributes set.
Direct beratungston: 'Wir lösen also nicht Iris weg, sondern Iris schlank machen.' No 'amazing', 'perfect', 'cool' — uses 'Restoration', 'Identitätsentscheidung', 'kanonische Stand'.

notes: Strong outcome. Recommendation 'tighten the iris, don't replace it' is exactly the design-system-aligned move. Reframes the user's performance complaint as accumulated complexity, not the iris concept. Explicitly walks through every wrong option (lens shader, filmstrip wipe, sequence stage, ken-burns static, flat image) and why each is wrong. Cites SKILL.md failure modes by name.
